page: Microsoft Azure F16ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv6 server family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, delivering 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The architecture is x86_64, featuring a cache configuration of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, translating to a high memory density of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing premium remote storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core and memory capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves top-tier performance, placing in the top 10%. Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are also strong, ranking in the top 25%, with text processing benchmarks reaching the top 10%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing also show strong performance in the top 25%. For language model workloads, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds of 352.45 tokens per second for a small 135M model, alongside strong prompt processing speeds. Other metrics, including PassMark CPU scores, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput, fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F16ams_v6 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio combined with dedicated physical cores, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that still require high single-core compute performance. The primary tradeoff is the complete absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, meaning storage-heavy or parallelized graphics workloads must rely on remote storage and CPU-based execution. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, text processing, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F16ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famsv6 family, powered by a 16-core dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it supports premium remote storage. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, alongside strong multi-core Geekbench results and efficient small-model LLM text generation. This configuration is optimized for memory-demanding workloads, database operations, text processing, and lightweight inference tasks. The absence of local NVMe storage represents a tradeoff, requiring remote storage solutions for data-intensive applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance, optimized for databases and text processing.
